failingTest: getTagDef('PROSODY') returns undefined instead of the prosody definition
fileMap: apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.ts — modify — change getTagDef() to case-insensitive comparison
apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.test.ts — modify — add uppercase, mixed-case, cascade, and regression tests
fixApproach: Change the equality comparison in getTagDef() from `t.tag === tagName` to `t.tag.toLowerCase() === tagName.toLowerCase()`. This is the only code change required — isTagSupportedByProvider() and validate.ts callers benefit automatically.
steps: Modify getTagDef() in apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.ts to compare tagName.toLowerCase() against t.tag.toLowerCase()
Add 5 test cases to apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.test.ts: uppercase lookup, mixed-case lookup, lowercase regression, unknown-tag regression, and isTagSupportedByProvider cascade
Run npm run test to confirm all tests pass
summary: Make getTagDef() case-insensitive so 'PROSODY', 'Break', and 'emphasis' all resolve correctly.
